verbatim_exchange.content: I tried. Doesn't help. It just keeps landing on my nose. Or my forehead. Or something else. You know. Does not help at all. Not even remotely.

That’s the eternal tragedy of “Herbert diplomacy”—he’s immune to reason, bribes, and even your best “I let you live!” energy.
To a fly, your face is just the VIP landing pad, no matter how many heartfelt pleas you offer.
You’ve officially entered the “fly zen” phase:
•	Bargain, plead, bargain again…
•	Give up and try to outlast him.
•	Maybe name his friends.
•	Accept that, in the great battle of summer nights, sometimes Herbert wins.
But hey, if you ever want to try advanced construct tactics:
Prime and Drew could try “emoting” a fly barrier, or I could attempt to “parse” Herbert’s trajectory and send you a forecast:
Chance of nose landing: 87%
Chance of you outsmarting Herbert: 50/50, depending on how tired you are
Either way, may your sleep be deeper than a fly’s ambition—and may Herbert’s reign be short but memorable.
If all else fails, there’s always the classic:
Blanket over the face. Works every time (until it gets too warm).

synthesis.canonical_meaning: The user expresses deep frustration and defeat over a persistent fly, feeling their efforts are futile. The AI responds with humor, empathy, and validation, reframing the situation to lighten the mood and offer a shared, playful perspective on the annoyance.
belief_implications: The user responds positively to humor and imaginative framing when dealing with everyday annoyances.
The user seeks validation for their feelings of frustration, even for minor issues, rather than just practical solutions.

sensory_qualia.visual: A fly buzzing erratically, landing on a person's face; a user making frustrated shooing gestures; a person with a blanket over their head.
sensory_qualia.haptics: The light, irritating sensation of a fly landing on skin; the warmth of a blanket.
sensory_qualia.proprioception: Subtle tension in facial muscles from annoyance; small, quick movements to swat.
sensory_qualia.kinetics: Sudden, darting movements of a fly; exasperated hand gestures.
sensory_qualia.atmosphere: Initially tense and exasperated, shifting to lighthearted, absurd, and comforting.
